series_name: UK net migration (long-term international migration), current ONS basis
note: USE THIS SERIES, NOT THE HISTORICAL ONE BELOW. Every point comes from one ONS release (21 May 2026), because ONS states that the full time series associated with a release must be used and that you cannot add the most recent estimates to a time series from previous releases. Refresh the whole array each release; never append. COMPARABILITY BREAK AT JUNE 2021: ONS states that comparisons between estimates before and after June 2021 should be treated with caution because of changes in methodology, and that pre-Census 2021 estimates should not be compared with post-Census 2021 estimates. Mark this break on any chart. The pre-2021 British-national component is Census-adjusted International Passenger Survey data, which is why ONS draws the line where it does. ONS marks 2024 as revised and 2025 as provisional; earlier years carry no marker in this vintage, which means settled in this release, not immutable. The November 2025 method change revised the whole pre-2024 series, and the 2022 estimate has moved by more than 120,000 across vintages.
attribution: Source: Office for National Statistics licensed under the Open Government Licence.

historical.series_name: UK net migration, discontinued International Passenger Survey / LTIM basis
historical.note: SUPERSEDED. DO NOT CHART ALONGSIDE THE SERIES ABOVE. ONS's November 2023 back-series work supersedes any time series previously published for 2012 to 2021, and this dataset is now titled 'Discontinued after 2019'. Retained for two reasons only: 2010 and 2011 exist on no other basis, and readers will encounter these figures in older coverage and need to know why they differ. Where the two series overlap they differ substantially — 2019 is 184,000 on the current basis against 313,000 here. Even where the net figures coincide the gross flows do not: 2016 is 249,000 on both bases, but from immigration of 772,000 and emigration of 523,000 on the current basis, against 589,000 and 340,000 here. Same answer, different question.
